briefing.title: Market Volume Leader, Not Value Leader
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores in units sold MTD (204) and sold value (₱44.46M), but avg ticket of ₱174,335 trails Shop with K (₱325,987), KC Luxury (₱316,058), and Luxury Wish (₱304,631), signaling a mix or pricing opportunity in ultra-premium SKUs.
briefing.action: Review the 'Other Other' and lower-tier Chanel listings pulling avg ticket down; reprice or replace with higher-value Hermès and exotic-hardware pieces to close the ticket gap vs top-tier competitors.

briefing.title: Miss Manila Luxe Velocity Threat
briefing.detail: Miss Manila Luxe turned 579 units MTD at avg 9.7 days-to-sell vs our 16.7 days — nearly 3× our unit velocity despite an avg ticket of only ₱70,454, dominating entry-level LV, Prada, Gucci, and Saint Laurent.
briefing.action: Assess our 'Other' brand inventory (29.1% of mix); if holding slow-moving entry-level pieces, either markdown aggressively or redirect buying budget away from that tier entirely since Miss Manila Luxe owns that segment.

briefing.title: Hermès Birkin/Kelly Sell-Through Strong
briefing.detail: Hermès is 39.3% of our mix and Birkin + Kelly appear as our #2 and #3 top-sold models MTD; market sell-through confirms demand — Birkin and Kelly appear in top-5 of 5 out of 7 active competitors.
briefing.action: Accelerate Hermès Birkin and Kelly restocking; current live counts (Birkin 25: 7, Birkin 30: 4, Kelly 25: 6, Kelly 28: 10) are thin relative to our 1,661-unit store size — prioritize sourcing in 25–30cm sizes.

briefing.title: Chanel Jumbo Overstock Risk
briefing.detail: We hold 25 Chanel Double Flap Jumbos — the single largest hero-model inventory position — against a total market live count of 40, meaning we represent 62.5% of all market Jumbo supply, with our lowest ask at ₱188,000 vs market low of ₱170,000.
briefing.action: Immediately drop at least 5 Jumbo units to ₱170,000–₱175,000 to match market floor and accelerate turns; do not acquire additional Jumbos until live count falls below 15.

briefing.title: Mini Kelly Scarcity — Premium Pricing Justified
briefing.detail: Only 4 Mini Kelly (Kelly 20) units exist market-wide; we hold 2 of them at ₱1,540,000 lowest ask vs market low of ₱970,000 and high of ₱1,680,000 — we are priced at the top of the range.
briefing.action: Hold current Mini Kelly pricing; at ₱1,540,000 we are within ₱140,000 of the market ceiling with limited competition — do not discount, and flag any incoming Mini Kelly sourcing opportunities as high-priority buys.

briefing.title: Luxury Wish — Small Store, Dangerous Sell-Through
briefing.detail: Luxury Wish holds only 168 units but sold 28 MTD (16.7% sell-through rate) at ₱304,631 avg ticket and the fastest days-to-sell among premium stores (14.1 days), suggesting very sharp pricing and curated Chanel-heavy inventory (47.2%).
briefing.action: Monitor Luxury Wish Chanel and Hermès Constance listings daily — they are our closest premium competitor by model overlap; match or undercut their price on identical SKUs where we have excess stock.

alerts: KC Luxury Bags and Mommy Micah both show 0 sales MTD — combined ₱53.2M inventory sitting idle; watch for imminent price dumps on Hermès and luxury 'Other' that could pressure our pricing.
Birkin 30: our lowest ask (₱728,000) is ₱208,000 above market low (₱520,000) — significant premium risk on 4 live units; review condition/hardware differentiation or consider selective markdown.
Kelly 28: market is deeply liquid (27 units live, floor at ₱480,000) and our floor is ₱735,000 — a ₱255,000 gap with no stated premium justification; high stagnation risk on our 10 units.

The Orange Box avg ticket (₱223,354) exceeds ours by ₱49,019 on lower volume — they are trading up successfully on Hermès (50.7% mix); if they begin acquiring Birkins aggressively, expect price floor pressure.

Constance 24: our floor (₱534,500) is ₱139,500 above market low (₱395,000) on a 17-unit liquid market — risk of aging if not adjusted; Constance 18 gap is tighter (₱695K vs ₱650K market low) and more defensible.

pricing.suggestion: Reduce lowest ask from ₱728,000 toward ₱580,000–₱620,000 to be competitive within the ₱520,000–₱2,980,000 market range; the current ₱208,000 premium over market floor is undefended and risks stagnation on all 4 units.

Drop floor pricing from ₱735,000 to ₱540,000–₱580,000 on standard hardware/condition units; with 27 market listings and a ₱480,000 floor, our 10-unit position is highly exposed — reserve ₱700K+ only for exotic or rare hardware.

Reprice 5+ units to ₱170,000–₱178,000 immediately to match the ₱170,000 market floor; at 25 units we hold 62.5% of total market supply and cannot sustain a premium — velocity must take priority over margin.

Selectively reduce 2–3 units to ₱420,000–₱450,000 to be competitive near the ₱395,000 market floor; reserve units with rare leathers or gold hardware at ₱534,500+ with clear merchandising justification.

Our floor of ₱1,150,000 sits ₱380,000 above market low (₱770,000) — audit the 6 live units; standard Togo/Epsom units should be repriced to ₱850,000–₱950,000 while exotic or limited editions can hold above ₱1.1M.

Hold at ₱1,540,000 — only 4 units exist market-wide and the ceiling is ₱1,680,000; this is a scarcity play and any discount would be value destruction, but do not price above ₱1,650,000 without exceptional provenance.
